sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The MET class, implemented in Cython, is a specialized selection template within the AnalysisG framework, designed for handling Missing Transverse Energy (MET) related selections. It inherits from the generic SelectionTemplate class, extending its functionalities to incorporate features and structures relevant to MET-based analyses. This class provides a high-performance interface to an underlying C++ MET object, enabling efficient filtering and analysis based on MET properties.

sect1.title: The MET Class
sect1.para: MET is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. It inherits from SelectionTemplate, thereby gaining all the base functionalities for event selection, ROOT file interpretation, and weight management. Its primary role is to encapsulate a C++ met object, which likely contains the logic for calculating and applying selections based on MET.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ met object and assigns its pointer to self.ptr. It then upcasts self.ptr to a met* and assigns it to self.tt for specialized access.
__init__(self): (Not explicitly defined in the provided snippet) This would typically be the Python initializer.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ met object pointed to by self.tt, ensuring proper memory management for the specialized MET data.

cdef void transform_dict_keys(self): This method is intended to transform the keys of internal dictionaries. In this specific implementation, it converts the missing_et member of the underlying C++ met object into a Python dictionary using as_basic_dict and assigns it to self.missing_et. This is likely used to expose MET values to the Python layer.
@property missing_et: (Implicitly defined through transform_dict_keys) This property would provide access to a dictionary containing MET-related values, converted from the underlying C++ met object.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The MET class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class MET: Declares MET as a Cython extension type.
cdef met *tt: A C++ pointer to a met object, which is the specialized C++ implementation for MET calculations and selections.
self.ptr = new met(): Initializes the base SelectionTemplate's C++ pointer.
self.tt = <met*>self.ptr: This line demonstrates C++ upcasting, where the base SelectionTemplate's C++ pointer (self.ptr) is cast to the specialized met type and assigned to self.tt. This allows MET to fulfill the SelectionTemplate interface while internally managing its specific met data.
from AnalysisG.core.selection_template cimport SelectionTemplate: Imports the base SelectionTemplate class, from which MET inherits.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports utility functions for converting between C++ and Python data structures, specifically as_basic_dict.
